titleStmt.title: Letter from James Brown to Matthew Nathan, 1 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: James Brown

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from solicitor James Brown to Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1964), under-secretary for Ireland, applying for the position of Chief Crown Solicitor, following the death of Sir Malachy Kelly.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5810/2

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Offices: Broad Street, Magherafelt 1 April 1916 Dear Sir I understand the position
of Chief Crown Solicitor for Ireland
is now vacant and I beg to
apply to you for the position and
I would like to know what is
salary attached to the position
and if appointed could I carry
on my practice in County Kerry.
As regards my qualifications
for the position the only gentle-
man who knows my capacity
as a criminal lawyer is the
present attorney General for
Ireland (M Gordan). Hoping
you will give my application
your best consideration. I remain Yours James Brown
To The UndersSec'y for Ireland.
